revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hachiman shrine]][[fa:معبد هاچیمان]][[fr:Sanctuaire Hachiman]][[it:Santuario di Hachiman]][[ja:八幡宮]][[simple:Hachiman shrines]][[vi:Đền thờ thần Hachiman]]
{{short description|Shinto shrine dedicated to Hachiman}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} [[Image:Iwashimizu hachimangu02s3200.jpg|275 px|thumb|right|[[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], a Hachiman shrine in {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|qid=Q260923|lt=Yawata|1=Yawata}}.]]
A {{Nihongo|'''Hachiman shrine'''|八幡神社|Hachiman Jinja|also {{nihongo3||八幡宮|Hachimangū}}}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]].<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776|title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions)|last=Motegi|first=Sadazumi|publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto|language=Japanese|accessdate=23 March 2010}}</ref> It is the second most numerous type of Shinto shrine after those dedicated to {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}} (see {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}).<ref name="bunpu"/> There are about 44,000 Hachiman shrines.
Originally the name 八幡 was read ''Yawata'' or ''Yahata'', a reading still used in some cases. Many towns and cities incorporating the names ''Hachiman'', ''Yawata'' or ''Yahata'' grew around these shrines.
==Famous Hachiman shrines== The following four shrines are often grouped into groups of three, either as Usa-Iwashimizu-Hakozaki or Usa-Iwashimizu-Tsurugaoka, and both of these groupings are known as the Three Major Hachiman Shrines of Japan ({{linktext|三|大|八幡|宮}}).
* [[Usa Jingū]] {{linktext|宇佐|神宮}} ({{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|qid=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}), the Sōhonsha (head shrine) * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] {{linktext|石清水|八幡|宮}} ({{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|qid=Q260923|lt=Yawata|1=Yawata}}) * [[Hakozaki Shrine]] {{linktext|筥崎|宮}} ({{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}}) * [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] {{linktext|鶴岡|八幡|宮}} ({{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}})
==Other notable Hachiman shrines== * [[Fukuyama Hachimangū]] 福山八幡宮 ({{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|qid=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama, Hiroshima|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}) * [[Hakodate Hachimangū|Hakodate Hachiman Shrine]] 函館八幡宮 ([[:en:Hakodate, Hokkaido|Hakodate, Hokkaido]]) * {{ill|Hatogamine Hachiman Shrine|en|Hatogamine Hachiman Shrine|qid=Q3128320|lt=Hatogamine Hachiman Shrine|1=Hatogamine Hachiman Shrine}} 鳩峰八幡神社 ({{ill|Tokorozawa, Saitama|en|Tokorozawa, Saitama|qid=Q165857|lt=Tokorozawa, Saitama|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}) * [[Iino Hachimangū]] 飯野八幡宮 ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|qid=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}) * [[Kameyama Hachimangū]] 亀山八幡宮 ({{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki|1=Shimonoseki}}, Yamaguchi) * [[Miyake Hachimangū]] 三宅八幡宮 ({{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321832|lt=Sakyo-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}) * [[Morioka Hachimangū]] 盛岡八幡宮 ({{ill|Morioka|en|Morioka|qid=Q200077|lt=Morioka|1=Morioka}}, Iwate) * [[Ōsaki Hachimangū]] 大崎八幡宮 ({{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai|1=Sendai}}, Miyagi) * {{ill|Shiraoi Hachiman Shrine|en|Shiraoi Hachiman Shrine|qid=Q109973508|lt=Shiraoi Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiraoi Hachiman Shrine}} 白老八幡神社 ({{ill|Shiraoi, Hokkaido|en|Shiraoi, Hokkaido|qid=Q1348760|lt=Shiraoi, Hokkaido|1=Shiraoi, Hokkaido}}) * [[Shiroyama Hachimangū]] 城山八幡宮 ({{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}) * [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]] 富岡八幡宮 ([[:en:Koto, Tokyo|Koto, Tokyo]]) * [[:en:Tamukeyama Hachiman Shrine|Tamukeyama Hachiman Shrine]] 手向山八幡宮 (near the [[Tōdai-ji]], {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}) * [[Umi Hachiman-gū]] 宇美八幡宮 ({{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|qid=Q945518|lt=Umi, Fukuoka|1=Umi, Fukuoka}}) * {{ill|Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)|en|Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)|qid=Q10934922|lt=Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)|1=Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)}} 大宮八幡宮 ({{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|qid=Q232631|lt=Suginami|1=Suginami}}, Tokyo) * [[Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Hyōgo)]] 大宮八幡宮 ({{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|qid=Q31238|lt=Miki, Hyōgo|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}) * [[:en:Ōshio Hachiman Shrine|Ōshio Hachiman Shrine]] 大塩八幡宮 ({{ill|Echizen, Fukui|en|Echizen, Fukui|qid=Q877941|lt=Echizen, Fukui|1=Echizen, Fukui}}) * {{ill|Sapporo Hachimangū|en|Sapporo Hachimangū|qid=Q28689612|lt=Sapporo Hachimangū|1=Sapporo Hachimangū}} 札幌八幡宮 [[:en:Sapporo, Hokkaido|Sapporo, Hokkaido]] * [[:en:Hachiman Jinja (Saipan)|Saipan Hachiman Shrine]] ([[:en:Saipan|Saipan]], {{ill|Northern Mariana Islands|en|Northern Mariana Islands|qid=Q16644|lt=Northern Mariana Islands|1=Northern Mariana Islands}})
== See also == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}
== References == <references/>
==External links== {{commons Category|Hachiman shrines}} * {{cite web |url=http://rossbender.org/usahachimangu.html |title=Usa Shrine 宇佐神宮 |accessdate=2008-05-25 |last=Bender |first=Ross |date= |work= |publisher= |archive-date=3 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303183223/http://rossbender.org/usahachimangu.html |url-status=dead }} * {{cite web |url=http://rossbender.org/miyazaki.html |title=Miyazaki Hachiman Shrine 宮崎八幡宮 |accessdate=2008-05-25 |last=Bender |first=Ross |date= |work= |publisher= |archive-date=3 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303184519/http://rossbender.org/miyazaki.html |url-status=dead }}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->
